mandate.title: HR Employee Onboarding Automation System
mandate.description: A web-based application that digitizes and automates the employee onboarding process for a 50-person company. The system will replace manual paper-based workflows with automated document generation, electronic signatures, task tracking, and notifications integrated with Google Workspace. Target: reduce onboarding time from 2 weeks to 3-5 days.
target_users: New Hires (form completion and document signing)
core_features: Onboarding workflow builder with customizable steps and tasks
Automated document generation from templates (offer letters, contracts, policies)
Google Workspace integration (Gmail for notifications, Drive for document storage, Calendar for scheduling)
Dashboard showing onboarding progress and status for all new hires
Automated email notifications and reminders to stakeholders
Document repository with version control and audit trail
Task checklist assignment and tracking (IT setup, manager introductions, etc.)
success_criteria: Reduce average onboarding time from 14 days to 5 days or less
Automate 90%+ of HR manager email communications during onboarding
Provide real-time visibility into status of all active onboarding processes
Reduce HR manager time spent per new hire from ~16 hours to under 4 hours
constraints: Budget: estimated $30K-$80K range (needs confirmation - marked as blocking uncertainty)
Must integrate with Google Workspace (existing infrastructure)
Small company scale: 50 employees, ~10 new hires per year
No dedicated IT team implied - system must be low-maintenance
Compliance requirements unknown - industry and regulatory context not specified
team_needs: Backend developer with workflow engine experience
UX/UI designer with HR workflow experience

tensions.wants: clear definition of what this HR onboarding app includes and excludes
tensions.value: Digital employee onboarding workflow system: automate document generation, electronic signatures, task tracking, and notification system. OUT OF SCOPE: full HRIS, payroll integration, benefits administration, performance management, applicant tracking.

doubts.about: What specific documents need to be generated (offer letters, tax forms, contracts, etc.)
Whether they need integration with existing systems beyond Google Workspace
Definition of 'flexible budget' - need concrete number to scope appropriately

tensions.wants: who will use this system daily

doubts.about: Whether IT/Admin needs access for configuration or troubleshooting
If CFO or other executives need visibility into onboarding status

tensions.wants: measurable outcomes that define project success
value: Reduce onboarding time from 2 weeks to 3-5 days
HR manager saves 10+ hours per new hire

doubts.about: Client hasn't specified compliance requirements (e.g., document retention, audit trails)
Whether there are specific onboarding quality metrics they care about beyond speed

tensions.wants: technical skills required to build this
value: Backend developer with workflow engine experience

doubts.about: Whether we need dedicated mobile development or if responsive web is sufficient
If compliance requirements emerge, may need legal tech specialist

tensions.wants: realistic timeline to deliver MVP
tensions.value: 12-16 weeks for MVP (core workflow, e-signatures, Google Workspace integration, basic document templates)

doubts.about: Timeline depends heavily on document template complexity and customization needs
E-signature integration complexity varies by provider and compliance needs
No information on client's urgency or deadline constraints

tensions.wants: high-level technical approach
tensions.value: Web-based SaaS application with Google Workspace OAuth, cloud-hosted (AWS/GCP), PostgreSQL for workflow state, third-party e-signature API, email via Gmail API

doubts.about: Whether client has preferences or restrictions on cloud providers
Data residency or security compliance requirements unknown
Whether they want self-hosted or cloud SaaS model

tensions.wants: concrete budget range to guide scoping decisions
tensions.value: Small company, 10 hires/year, 'flexible but not crazy' suggests $30K-$80K range for initial build

doubts.about: No concrete budget number provided - this is pure inference from company size
Unclear if budget includes ongoing maintenance and hosting costs
Third-party costs (e-signature API, hosting) not discussed

tensions.wants: understanding of legal and compliance requirements

doubts.about: Industry not specified - may have sector-specific compliance (healthcare, finance, etc.)
Document retention policies and audit trail requirements unknown
Whether I-9 verification or other government forms are required
Data privacy requirements (GDPR, CCPA) depending on employee locations

tech_stack.backend: Node.js via Next.js API routes (unified full-stack framework)
tech_stack.rationale: Next.js 14 provides unified full-stack TypeScript development with strong type safety, excellent developer experience, and natural fit for SaaS applications. Google Workspace OAuth integration is first-class via next-auth. PostgreSQL offers robust relational modeling for workflow state, ACID guarantees for sensitive HR data, and row-level security for multi-tenant isolation. This stack is independent of compliance requirements - the core framework choice does not depend on specific security or regulatory constraints, though additional libraries (audit logging, encryption) may be added based on compliance discovery.

deployment_model.hosting: Vercel (frontend + API routes) with Neon or Supabase PostgreSQL
deployment_model.approach: Cloud SaaS, serverless functions for API, managed PostgreSQL with connection pooling, preview deployments for staging/review, environment-based secrets management
deployment_model.rationale: Vercel provides native Next.js optimization, edge caching, and zero-config deployments. Neon/Supabase offer managed PostgreSQL with branching, backups, and scaling without ops overhead. This assumes cloud SaaS is acceptable per CEO's technical_architecture (0.7 confidence) but with honest uncertainty: data residency is unknown (compliance_and_legal at 0.3), and self-hosted preference is not established. Hosting costs ($50-200/month) are modest but not confirmed within budget. This is a reasoned choice that advances the project but carries medium deployment risk if compliance or hosting preferences shift.

vendors.rationale: Already required for authentication per project scope. Avoids third-party email service costs. Suitable for notification volume (10 hires/year = low volume). May need transactional service (SendGrid, Resend) if volume scales or delivery guarantees become critical.

Sentry free tier supports early-stage error tracking and performance monitoring. Vercel Analytics is included with hosting. If compliance requires data residency controls or paid tier features, can migrate to self-hosted Sentry or alternative. Low cost, low risk, enables production observability from day one.

deferred.blocked_by: compliance_and_legal (0.3 confidence) and budget_constraints (0.5 confidence, contested). Legal validity requirements vary by jurisdiction and industry - unknown if ESIGN/UETA sufficiency or if specific certifications needed (I-9, government forms). Cost structures range dramatically: DocuSign $40-60/user/month, HelloSign/Dropbox Sign API $125/month flat rate, PandaDoc $19-49/user/month. Cannot choose responsibly without compliance requirements and explicit budget.

deferred.decision: Document storage provider (Vercel Blob vs Cloudflare R2 vs S3)
deferred.blocked_by: compliance_and_legal (0.3 confidence). Data residency requirements, retention policies, encryption at rest standards, and access control requirements all unknown. While volume is low (10 hires/year), regulatory requirements may dictate specific storage architecture. Technical choice depends on legal constraints.

deferred.decision: Security model depth (encryption at rest, audit logging architecture)
deferred.blocked_by: compliance_and_legal (0.3 confidence). Baseline security (OAuth, HTTPS, RLS) can be implemented, but encryption at rest for PII fields, immutable audit trail architecture, document retention lifecycle, and potential SOC 2 requirements cannot be scoped without compliance discovery.

project_scope (0.7 confidence) has medium doubt about specific documents needed. Offer letters vs tax forms vs government forms have different generation complexity. Simple templating (react-email + PDF render) vs structured form generation (pdf-lib, PDFKit) vs third-party service (DocuSpring, Carbone) depends on document types and customization needs.

blueprint.summary: We are building a digital employee onboarding workflow system for a small company (~10 hires/year) to automate document generation, electronic signatures, task tracking, and notifications. The CTO has decided on Next.js 14 with TypeScript (unified frontend/backend), PostgreSQL 15+, and Google Workspace integration for authentication and email notifications. Key libraries include next-auth, Prisma ORM, react-hook-form, Zod, and TailwindCSS. The system will be web-based SaaS targeting three user roles: HR Managers (primary users), New Hires (document signing), and Department Managers (approvals). Critical blockers remain around e-signature vendor selection, document storage strategy, deployment hosting specifics, and compliance requirements.

components.name: Next.js Application
components.responsibility: User interface for HR dashboard, employee onboarding portal, document management, and task tracking. Includes all frontend routes and server-side API routes.

components.name: Authentication Module
components.responsibility: Google OAuth integration via next-auth, session management with JWT, role-based access control (HR_ADMIN, MANAGER, EMPLOYEE), middleware-based route protection.

components.name: Workflow Engine
components.responsibility: Orchestrates onboarding workflow state machine, manages task dependencies and transitions, triggers time-based rules, handles manual overrides. Implements business logic for workflow progression.

components.name: Document Service
components.responsibility: Generates documents from templates with variable interpolation, stores document metadata, retrieves documents, handles PDF generation (react-pdf or pdf-lib), provides HTML preview.

components.name: E-signature Integration
components.responsibility: Wrapper for third-party e-signature provider SDK, initiates signature requests, handles webhook callbacks for signature completion, manages signature status updates.

components.name: Notification Service
components.responsibility: Sends email notifications via Gmail API using Google Workspace OAuth, manages notification templates, tracks notification delivery status, queues notifications.

components.name: Database Layer
components.responsibility: Prisma ORM abstraction over PostgreSQL, defines schema for all entities (Employee, OnboardingWorkflow, WorkflowTask, Document, DocumentTemplate, Notification), handles migrations and queries.

components.name: Document Storage
components.responsibility: Cloud object storage for generated documents and uploaded files (Vercel Blob or AWS S3), manages file uploads/downloads, handles access control and encryption.

components.name: Background Job Processor
components.responsibility: Scheduled tasks for workflow reminders, deadline enforcement, notification batching. Implementation depends on deployment model (Vercel Cron vs dedicated worker).

components.name: PostgreSQL Database
components.responsibility: Persistent storage for all application data including workflows, tasks, documents metadata, templates, users, notifications. Serverless PostgreSQL via Neon or similar.

data_model.notes: Authenticated via Google OAuth. Role determines access permissions. HR_ADMIN can manage templates and workflows, MANAGER receives approvals, EMPLOYEE views own onboarding.

relations: belongs to User (optional, linked after account creation)
data_model.notes: Employee record can exist before User account is created. Links to User once they authenticate for first time. Department stored as string initially, may become relation if org structure needed.

fields: status (enum: DRAFT, ACTIVE, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, CANCELLED)
data_model.notes: Central orchestration entity. Status transitions managed by Workflow Engine. Due date calculated from employee start_date. Tasks and documents linked for progress tracking.

fields: type (enum: DOCUMENT_GENERATION, SIGNATURE_REQUEST, APPROVAL, SYSTEM_SETUP, CUSTOM)
relations: may have one Document (if type is DOCUMENT_GENERATION or SIGNATURE_REQUEST)
data_model.notes: Tasks execute based on order and prerequisites. Assignee_type determines who sees the task. System tasks execute automatically. Order field enables sequential dependencies.

fields: category (enum: OFFER_LETTER, CONTRACT, HANDBOOK, TAX_FORM, CUSTOM)
data_model.notes: Template variables stored as JSON schema defining required fields for interpolation. Content_template format depends on document generation approach (Handlebars vs template literals). Complexity blocked on project_scope clarity.

relations: belongs to DocumentTemplate (if generated from template)
data_model.notes: Storage_url points to Document Storage. Signature fields depend on vendor_esignature choice. Provider_metadata stores vendor-specific data for status tracking. May need audit trail relation depending on compliance_and_legal.

fields: type (enum: TASK_ASSIGNED, TASK_REMINDER, SIGNATURE_REQUEST, WORKFLOW_COMPLETE, APPROVAL_NEEDED)
relations: may relate to WorkflowTask via metadata
may relate to OnboardingWorkflow via metadata
data_model.notes: Email sent via Gmail API. Status tracks delivery. Metadata stores context (workflow_id, task_id) for linking. Notification templates managed in code initially, may move to database if customization needed.

api_contracts.purpose: Create new onboarding workflow for an employee. HR Admin initiates workflow with employee details and due date.
Fetch workflow details including tasks, documents, and current status. Used by HR dashboard and employee portal.
api_contracts.auth: Google OAuth required, role-based filtering (HR sees all, employee sees own)

api_contracts.purpose: Update workflow status, due date, or other metadata. Used for manual overrides by HR Admin.
Update task status (mark complete, skip, reassign). Called by task assignee or HR Admin.
api_contracts.auth: Google OAuth required, task assignee or HR_ADMIN role

api_contracts.purpose: Generate document from template with provided variables. Creates Document record and triggers PDF generation.
api_contracts.auth: Google OAuth required, HR_ADMIN or MANAGER role

api_contracts.purpose: Initiate e-signature request for a document. Calls e-signature provider API and creates signature request.
Retrieve document content or metadata. Returns storage URL for download or inline viewing.
api_contracts.auth: Google OAuth required, role-based access (workflow participant only)

api_contracts.purpose: Webhook endpoint for e-signature provider callbacks. Updates document and task status when signature completes.
List employees with filters (status, department, date range). Used by HR dashboard for overview.
api_contracts.auth: Google OAuth required, HR_ADMIN or MANAGER role

api_contracts.purpose: Create new employee record. First step before creating workflow.
List all active document templates. Used by HR when creating workflows or generating documents.
Create or update document template. Admin function for template management.
Fetch user's notification inbox. Shows pending tasks and workflow updates.

risks.description: CTO has not decided on e-signature vendor (vendor_esignature confidence 0.1 with blocking doubts). Provider choice affects API contract design, document workflow, compliance capabilities, and cost structure. DocuSign, HelloSign, and Dropbox Sign have different pricing ($1.5K-$7K annually) and feature sets.
risks.mitigation: Cannot finalize Document Service or E-signature Integration component design until vendor decided. Blockers list includes document and API contract specifics. CEO must clarify budget and compliance requirements to enable CTO decision.

risks.description: Unknown whether document templates need simple text substitution, complex conditional logic, WYSIWYG editor, or government form field mapping. This affects Document Service design, development timeline (could add 2-4 weeks), and potentially requires specialized libraries or services.
risks.mitigation: CEO clarification needed on specific document types (offer letters, tax forms, I-9, etc.). For MVP, recommend starting with simple template approach and iterating. Document arch_document_generation as partial (0.6 confidence) with blocking doubt.

risks.description: CTO deployment_model tension remains at 0.1 confidence. Without concrete hosting decision, cannot finalize infrastructure setup, background job strategy, or cost estimates. Vercel + Neon assumed but not confirmed.
risks.mitigation: Architect has proposed Vercel + Neon + Vercel Blob in arch_deployment_config (0.6 confidence) as reasonable default given tech stack. Needs CTO confirmation. If data residency or self-hosting required, could require architecture rework.

risks.description: compliance_and_legal at 0.3 confidence. Unknown if GDPR, CCPA, SOC 2, industry-specific regulations apply. Affects data model (audit trails), security implementation (encryption at rest), document storage (retention policies), and potentially vendor choices.
risks.mitigation: Assume basic security best practices (HTTPS, OAuth, encrypted storage). Design extensible audit logging hooks. If strict compliance emerges, may need architecture additions (immutable audit log, field-level encryption). Add 2-3 week buffer if compliance requirements surface.

risks.description: CEO provided 'flexible but not crazy' guidance. CTO correctly challenged this (cto_challenge_budget_constraints) as insufficient for vendor selection. E-signature alone ranges $1.5K-$7K/year, hosting $600-$2.4K/year, monitoring $0-$1K/year.
risks.mitigation: Cannot select optimal vendors without budget clarity. CEO must provide concrete budget range. For now, design assumes budget-conscious choices (Neon free/hobby tier, Vercel hobby/pro, open-source monitoring) but vendors remain TBD.

risks.description: Vercel Cron has limitations (max 1/minute frequency, no complex scheduling, cold starts). For workflow reminders and deadline enforcement, may need dedicated worker service (adds infrastructure complexity) or accept Cron limitations.
risks.mitigation: For MVP, Vercel Cron likely sufficient (10 hires/year = low volume). Design Background Job Processor as abstraction to allow future migration to dedicated service (BullMQ, Inngest) if needed. Mark component confidence at 0.55.

risks.description: CTO document_storage tension at 0.1 confidence. Vercel Blob vs AWS S3 vs GCS affects Document Service API, deployment dependencies, and cost. Data residency requirements may constrain choice.
risks.mitigation: Architect proposes Vercel Blob for simplicity given Vercel deployment assumption, with S3 as fallback. Document Service should abstract storage provider (use consistent interface) to allow swap without major refactor. Mark Document Service confidence at 0.6.

risks.description: CTO noted uncertainty about mobile-native vs responsive web. New hires may need to sign documents on mobile devices. Responsive web is simpler but may have UX limitations for document signing and form completion.
risks.mitigation: Design mobile-responsive UI with TailwindCSS. Test document signing flow on mobile browsers early. If poor UX emerges, can add React Native wrapper in future iteration. Do not block MVP on native mobile app.

blockers.blocked_by: vendor_esignature - CTO has not selected e-signature provider (confidence 0.1, blocking doubts about compliance, budget, document types)

blockers.decision: Document entity signature-related fields (signature_request_id, provider_metadata structure)
blockers.blocked_by: vendor_esignature - field structure varies by provider (DocuSign vs HelloSign vs Dropbox Sign)
vendor_esignature - webhook signature verification and payload format are provider-specific

blockers.decision: Document Storage component vendor and configuration
blockers.blocked_by: document_storage - CTO has not decided between Vercel Blob, AWS S3, GCS (confidence 0.1)
document_storage - storage SDK and API differ between Vercel Blob (fetch-based) vs S3 (AWS SDK)

blockers.decision: DocumentTemplate content_template format and variable system
blockers.blocked_by: project_scope - document complexity unknown (simple substitution vs conditional logic vs WYSIWYG), marked as blocking doubt in arch_document_generation

blockers.decision: Document generation library choice (react-pdf vs pdf-lib vs headless browser)
blockers.blocked_by: project_scope - depends on template complexity and whether HTML rendering or programmatic PDF generation is needed

blockers.decision: Audit trail entity and logging implementation
blockers.blocked_by: compliance_and_legal - audit requirements unknown (confidence 0.3), affects data model and component cross-cutting concerns

blockers.decision: Encryption at rest implementation (application-level field encryption)
blockers.blocked_by: compliance_and_legal - PII encryption requirements not specified, may need crypto library or database-level encryption

blockers.decision: Background Job Processor implementation (Vercel Cron vs dedicated worker)
blockers.blocked_by: deployment_model - CTO has not finalized hosting strategy (confidence 0.1), affects infrastructure setup
vendor_monitoring - CTO has not decided on monitoring solution (confidence 0.1)

blockers.decision: Data residency configuration for PostgreSQL and document storage
blockers.blocked_by: compliance_and_legal - unknown if EU, specific region, or US acceptable for data storage

blockers.decision: Precise budget allocation for third-party services
blockers.blocked_by: budget_constraints - CEO has not provided concrete budget, CTO flagged as blocking issue

audit.verdict_rationale: The pipeline demonstrates coherence in core scope and technical decisions, with no fundamental contradictions that invalidate the Field. However, unresolved blocking doubts on budget and compliance are causing cascading deferrals in vendor selections and architecture components, requiring client input before proceeding to presentation.

inconsistencies.description: CEO inferred a budget range based on company size, while CTO flagged this as blocking due to lack of concrete client confirmation, creating a deadlock in vendor and scope decisions.
E-signature vendor selection is blocked by unknown budget, despite partial budget inference, leading to Architect deferring integration design.

false_blockers.reason: Doubts about GDPR or CCPA applicability are hypothetical rather than real, as the client is a US company with no evidence of international employee data handling requirements.

scope_reality_check.assessment: The MVP scope (workflow automation, document generation, e-signatures for 10 hires/year) aligns well with the inferred $30K-$80K budget, assuming mid-tier vendors and standard compliance.

discovery_questions.question: What is your budget for developing this onboarding system?
What industry is your company in, and are there any specific compliance or legal requirements we need to consider for employee documents?
What specific documents do you need to generate and have signed during onboarding?
Do you have any urgency or deadline for when you need this system ready?
Are there any existing systems or tools you want to integrate with beyond Google Workspace?
What are the key outcomes or metrics that will make this project successful for you?
Do you prefer the system to be cloud-hosted or self-hosted?
What roles need access to the system, and what can each role do?

qa.reasoning_raw:
## Understanding the Process  
- The process starts by calling read_field to analyze the Field and build a mental map of key decisions.  
- This includes what the CEO decided, CTO's responses, and Architect's actions, noting solid versus speculative reasoning.  

## Documenting Audit Tensions  
- Next, call update_field with findings, using qa_ prefixes for IDs, and focus on 3-5 tensions.  
- Tensions cover pipeline coherence, scope feasibility, and specific issues like blocking contradictions, without modifying others' work.  

## Generating the Audit Report  
- Fill the AuditReport schema, giving a verdict (green, yellow, red) based on rules and identifying inconsistencies.  
- Include false blockers, scope-to-budget comparisons, and up to 8 non-technical discovery questions, prioritized by importance.

---

## Reviewing Tech Stack
- The tech stack and basic architecture are solid, providing a stable foundation for the project.
- Dependencies like data models and document storage are unresolved, delaying full design completion.

## Identifying Tensions
- Budget and compliance issues are creating active tensions, with e-signature integration blocked due to budget constraints.
- The CTO has challenged budget allocations, adding uncertainty and deferring decisions on vendor selections.

## Evaluating QA Tensions
- QA pipeline coherence is affected by unresolved budget and compliance, causing cascading delays in the project.
- Scope feasibility seems reasonable for a $30-80k budget, but alignment is unclear without confirmed numbers.
- Budget inference is flagging as a blocker, potentially delaying vendor selections unnecessarily.
- Compliance speculation, possibly irrelevant for a likely US-based client, needs clarification to reduce hypothetical concerns.

## Assessing Project Verdict
- The project shows minor issues with unresolved budget and compliance, suggesting a yellow verdict rather than red.
- No fundamental contradiction exists, but budget uncertainty is a significant blocker affecting progress.

---

## Assessing Audit Report  
- The verdict is yellow, indicating minor issues like unresolved budget and compliance, but no fundamental contradictions affecting reliability.  
- The pipeline is coherent and just needs client input to move forward.  

## Analyzing Inconsistencies  
- Conflicts exist between the CEO's partial budget inference and the CTO's active challenge, both related to budget constraints.  
- The architect deferred vendor e-signature due to budget issues, showing budget constraints as a central inconsistency.  

## Evaluating False Blockers  
- Compliance concerns, like GDPR, may be speculative if the client is in the US, but the field notes dependence on employee locations, lowering severity.  
- To be conservative, no false blockers are confirmed, given the uncertainty around client location and industry.  

## Checking Scope and Budget  
- The MVP scope aligns with a $30K-$80K budget for a small company, but confirmation is needed, with confidence at 0.6.  
- Budget and scope alignment suggests the project is feasible, pending client verification.  

## Prioritizing Discovery Questions  
- Top critical question: "What is your budget for developing this onboarding system?" to unblock budget-related constraints.  
- High-priority questions include industry compliance and specific document needs to clarify legal and scope issues.

Gmail API sending limits per Workspace tier unknown - may need fallback to transactional email service
Whether Google Drive storage integration is desired vs dedicated object storage

tensions.wants: concrete hosting and infrastructure setup
tensions.value: Vercel for Next.js hosting (serverless), Neon for PostgreSQL (serverless, scales to zero), Vercel Blob or AWS S3 for document storage, environment-based configuration (dev/staging/prod), GitHub Actions for CI/CD

doubts.about: Deployment model tension still at confidence 0.1 - CTO has not decided on cloud provider or hosting specifics
Data residency requirements unknown - Neon region selection depends on compliance_and_legal
Background job execution strategy unclear - Vercel Cron has limitations for complex scheduling

tensions.wants: user authentication and role-based access control

doubts.about: Whether more granular permissions needed beyond three roles (e.g., read-only auditor)
Session duration and refresh strategy not specified - default vs custom expiry

tensions.wants: how document templates are stored, filled, and rendered
tensions.value: Template storage in PostgreSQL (DocumentTemplate table), variable interpolation using template literals or Handlebars, PDF generation via react-pdf or pdf-lib, HTML preview for web viewing, template variables defined per document type

doubts.about: Document template complexity unknown - simple text substitution vs complex conditional logic vs WYSIWYG editor
Whether HR needs template builder UI or templates are developer-maintained
Government form handling (I-9, W-4) may require structured field mapping vs template approach

tensions.wants: workflow lifecycle and task progression logic
tensions.value: OnboardingWorkflow states: DRAFT, ACTIVE, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, CANCELLED. WorkflowTask states: PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, SKIPPED. State transitions triggered by task completion, time-based rules, manual overrides. Task dependencies via order field and prerequisite logic.

doubts.about: Whether parallel task execution is needed or strictly sequential workflow
Approval workflow complexity - simple notification or blocking approval gates
Error handling states (e.g., SIGNATURE_FAILED) depend on integration error scenarios

tensions.wants: overall coherence of the pipeline output
tensions.value: The pipeline has strong coherence in core technical decisions and scope definition, but is hindered by cascading deferrals due to unresolved budget and compliance uncertainties, leading to speculative blocking doubts.

doubts.about: CTO's challenge on budget creates a deadlock where vendor selections cannot proceed without client input, which is reasonable but stalls the Architect.

tensions.wants: whether the project scope is realistic given budget signals

doubts.about: Budget is purely inferred from company size; actual number could misalign if lower or higher.

tensions.wants: assessment if budget inference is unnecessarily blocking decisions
tensions.value: The budget constraint is flagged as blocking due to lack of concrete number, but agents could proceed with a decision tree based on the inferred range rather than deferring entirely.

doubts.about: Without client confirmation, proceeding on inference risks scope creep or under-delivery.

tensions.wants: whether compliance doubts are overly speculative

doubts.about: Industry is unspecified, so speculation on compliance could miss critical requirements.

report.internal:
# HR Employee Onboarding Automation System

> A web-based application that digitizes and automates the employee onboarding process for a 50-person company. The system will replace manual paper-based workflows with automated document generation, electronic signatures, task tracking, and notifications integrated with Google Workspace. Target: reduce onboarding time from 2 weeks to 3-5 days.

**Project ID:** `proj-y-OvDiYK` · **Pipeline:** 376.1s · **Tokens:** 110 493 · **Field confidence:** 14%

---

## ◑ YELLOW

The pipeline demonstrates coherence in core scope and technical decisions, with no fundamental contradictions that invalidate the Field. However, unresolved blocking doubts on budget and compliance are causing cascading deferrals in vendor selections and architecture components, requiring client input before proceeding to presentation.

## Mandate

**Complexity:** medium

**Target users**
- HR Manager (primary admin user)
- New Hires (form completion and document signing)
- Department Managers (approvals and notifications)

**Core features**
- Onboarding workflow builder with customizable steps and tasks
- Automated document generation from templates (offer letters, contracts, policies)
- Electronic signature integration (DocuSign or HelloSign)
- Google Workspace integration (Gmail for notifications, Drive for document storage, Calendar for scheduling)
- Dashboard showing onboarding progress and status for all new hires
- Automated email notifications and reminders to stakeholders
- Document repository with version control and audit trail
- Task checklist assignment and tracking (IT setup, manager introductions, etc.)

**Success criteria**
- Reduce average onboarding time from 14 days to 5 days or less
- Eliminate 100% of paper-based document handling
- Automate 90%+ of HR manager email communications during onboarding
- Provide real-time visibility into status of all active onboarding processes
- Reduce HR manager time spent per new hire from ~16 hours to under 4 hours

**Constraints**
- Budget: estimated $30K-$80K range (needs confirmation - marked as blocking uncertainty)
- Must integrate with Google Workspace (existing infrastructure)
- Small company scale: 50 employees, ~10 new hires per year
- No dedicated IT team implied - system must be low-maintenance
- Compliance requirements unknown - industry and regulatory context not specified

**Team needs**
- Full-stack web developer (React/Vue + Node.js/Python)
- Backend developer with workflow engine experience
- Google Workspace API integration specialist
- Third-party API integration (e-signature provider)
- UX/UI designer with HR workflow experience
- QA engineer for workflow testing

## Technical Decisions

### Stack

| Layer | Choice |
|-------|--------|
| Frontend | Next.js 14 with React 18 and TypeScript |
| Backend | Node.js via Next.js API routes (unified full-stack framework) |
| Database | PostgreSQL 15+ |
| Key libs | next-auth v5 (Google Workspace OAuth), prisma (ORM, migrations, type safety), react-hook-form + zod (type-safe form validation), tailwindcss (utility-first styling), react-email (templated notification emails), @vercel/postgres or similar driver |

*Confidence: 75% — Next.js 14 provides unified full-stack TypeScript development with strong type safety, excellent developer experience, and natural fit for SaaS applications. Google Workspace OAuth integration is first-class via next-auth. PostgreSQL offers robust relational modeling for workflow state, ACID guarantees for sensitive HR data, and row-level security for multi-tenant isolation. This stack is independent of compliance requirements - the core framework choice does not depend on specific security or regulatory constraints, though additional libraries (audit logging, encryption) may be added based on compliance discovery.*

### Deployment

**Hosting:** Vercel (frontend + API routes) with Neon or Supabase PostgreSQL · **Approach:** Cloud SaaS, serverless functions for API, managed PostgreSQL with connection pooling, preview deployments for staging/review, environment-based secrets management

*Confidence: 65% — Vercel provides native Next.js optimization, edge caching, and zero-config deployments. Neon/Supabase offer managed PostgreSQL with branching, backups, and scaling without ops overhead. This assumes cloud SaaS is acceptable per CEO's technical_architecture (0.7 confidence) but with honest uncertainty: data residency is unknown (compliance_and_legal at 0.3), and self-hosted preference is not established. Hosting costs ($50-200/month) are modest but not confirmed within budget. This is a reasoned choice that advances the project but carries medium deployment risk if compliance or hosting preferences shift.*

### Vendors

| Category | Recommendation | Decision | Confidence |
|----------|---------------|----------|------------|
| email | Gmail API via Google Workspace | chosen | 80% |
| monitoring | Sentry (free tier) + Vercel Analytics | chosen | 70% |

### Deferred decisions

- **E-signature vendor selection** — blocked by `compliance_and_legal (0.3 confidence) and budget_constraints (0.5 confidence, contested). Legal validity requirements vary by jurisdiction and industry - unknown if ESIGN/UETA sufficiency or if specific certifications needed (I-9, government forms). Cost structures range dramatically: DocuSign $40-60/user/month, HelloSign/Dropbox Sign API $125/month flat rate, PandaDoc $19-49/user/month. Cannot choose responsibly without compliance requirements and explicit budget.`
- **Document storage provider (Vercel Blob vs Cloudflare R2 vs S3)** — blocked by `compliance_and_legal (0.3 confidence). Data residency requirements, retention policies, encryption at rest standards, and access control requirements all unknown. While volume is low (10 hires/year), regulatory requirements may dictate specific storage architecture. Technical choice depends on legal constraints.`
- **Security model depth (encryption at rest, audit logging architecture)** — blocked by `compliance_and_legal (0.3 confidence). Baseline security (OAuth, HTTPS, RLS) can be implemented, but encryption at rest for PII fields, immutable audit trail architecture, document retention lifecycle, and potential SOC 2 requirements cannot be scoped without compliance discovery.`
- **Document generation library/approach** — blocked by `project_scope (0.7 confidence) has medium doubt about specific documents needed. Offer letters vs tax forms vs government forms have different generation complexity. Simple templating (react-email + PDF render) vs structured form generation (pdf-lib, PDFKit) vs third-party service (DocuSpring, Carbone) depends on document types and customization needs.`

## Architecture

We are building a digital employee onboarding workflow system for a small company (~10 hires/year) to automate document generation, electronic signatures, task tracking, and notifications. The CTO has decided on Next.js 14 with TypeScript (unified frontend/backend), PostgreSQL 15+, and Google Workspace integration for authentication and email notifications. Key libraries include next-auth, Prisma ORM, react-hook-form, Zod, and TailwindCSS. The system will be web-based SaaS targeting three user roles: HR Managers (primary users), New Hires (document signing), and Department Managers (approvals). Critical blockers remain around e-signature vendor selection, document storage strategy, deployment hosting specifics, and compliance requirements.

### Components

| Name | Type | Responsibility | Confidence |
|------|------|----------------|------------|
| Next.js Application | frontend | User interface for HR dashboard, employee onboarding portal, document management, and task tracking. Includes all frontend routes and server-side API routes. | 90% |
| Authentication Module | backend | Google OAuth integration via next-auth, session management with JWT, role-based access control (HR_ADMIN, MANAGER, EMPLOYEE), middleware-based route protection. | 85% |
| Workflow Engine | backend | Orchestrates onboarding workflow state machine, manages task dependencies and transitions, triggers time-based rules, handles manual overrides. Implements business logic for workflow progression. | 75% |
| Document Service | backend | Generates documents from templates with variable interpolation, stores document metadata, retrieves documents, handles PDF generation (react-pdf or pdf-lib), provides HTML preview. | 60% |
| Notification Service | backend | Sends email notifications via Gmail API using Google Workspace OAuth, manages notification templates, tracks notification delivery status, queues notifications. | 80% |
| Database Layer | database | Prisma ORM abstraction over PostgreSQL, defines schema for all entities (Employee, OnboardingWorkflow, WorkflowTask, Document, DocumentTemplate, Notification), handles migrations and queries. | 85% |
| PostgreSQL Database | database | Persistent storage for all application data including workflows, tasks, documents metadata, templates, users, notifications. Serverless PostgreSQL via Neon or similar. | 75% |

### Data model

**User** (85%)
Fields: `id (uuid)`, `email (unique)`, `name`, `google_id (unique)`, `role (enum: HR_ADMIN, MANAGER, EMPLOYEE)`, `created_at`, `updated_at`
Relations: has many OnboardingWorkflows (as creator) · has many Notifications
*Authenticated via Google OAuth. Role determines access permissions. HR_ADMIN can manage templates and workflows, MANAGER receives approvals, EMPLOYEE views own onboarding.*

**Employee** (75%)
Fields: `id (uuid)`, `user_id (nullable, FK)`, `name`, `email`, `department`, `job_title`, `start_date`, `status (enum: PENDING, ACTIVE, COMPLETED, INACTIVE)`, `created_at`, `updated_at`
Relations: belongs to User (optional, linked after account creation) · has one OnboardingWorkflow
*Employee record can exist before User account is created. Links to User once they authenticate for first time. Department stored as string initially, may become relation if org structure needed.*

**OnboardingWorkflow** (80%)
Fields: `id (uuid)`, `employee_id (FK)`, `created_by (FK to User)`, `status (enum: DRAFT, ACTIVE, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, CANCELLED)`, `due_date`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `completed_at`
Relations: belongs to Employee · belongs to User (creator) · has many WorkflowTasks · has many Documents
*Central orchestration entity. Status transitions managed by Workflow Engine. Due date calculated from employee start_date. Tasks and documents linked for progress tracking.*

**WorkflowTask** (80%)
Fields: `id (uuid)`, `workflow_id (FK)`, `title`, `description`, `type (enum: DOCUMENT_GENERATION, SIGNATURE_REQUEST, APPROVAL, SYSTEM_SETUP, CUSTOM)`, `status (enum: PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, SKIPPED)`, `assignee_type (enum: HR, MANAGER, EMPLOYEE, SYSTEM)`, `assignee_email (nullable)`, `order`, `due_date`, `completed_at`, `completed_by (FK to User, nullable)`, `created_at`, `updated_at`
Relations: belongs to OnboardingWorkflow · may have one Document (if type is DOCUMENT_GENERATION or SIGNATURE_REQUEST)
*Tasks execute based on order and prerequisites. Assignee_type determines who sees the task. System tasks execute automatically. Order field enables sequential dependencies.*

**DocumentTemplate** (60%)
Fields: `id (uuid)`, `name`, `category (enum: OFFER_LETTER, CONTRACT, HANDBOOK, TAX_FORM, CUSTOM)`, `content_template (text)`, `variables (json)`, `is_active (boolean)`, `created_by (FK to User)`, `created_at`, `updated_at`
Relations: has many Documents (generated instances)
*Template variables stored as JSON schema defining required fields for interpolation. Content_template format depends on document generation approach (Handlebars vs template literals). Complexity blocked on project_scope clarity.*

**Document** (70%)
Fields: `id (uuid)`, `workflow_id (FK)`, `task_id (FK, nullable)`, `template_id (FK, nullable)`, `type`, `name`, `storage_url`, `status (enum: DRAFT, PENDING_SIGNATURE, SIGNED, CANCELLED)`, `signature_request_id (nullable)`, `signature_provider_metadata (json, nullable)`, `signed_at`, `signed_by (email)`, `created_at`, `updated_at`
Relations: belongs to OnboardingWorkflow · belongs to WorkflowTask (optional) · belongs to DocumentTemplate (if generated from template)
*Storage_url points to Document Storage. Signature fields depend on vendor_esignature choice. Provider_metadata stores vendor-specific data for status tracking. May need audit trail relation depending on compliance_and_legal.*

**Notification** (75%)
Fields: `id (uuid)`, `user_email`, `type (enum: TASK_ASSIGNED, TASK_REMINDER, SIGNATURE_REQUEST, WORKFLOW_COMPLETE, APPROVAL_NEEDED)`, `subject`, `body`, `status (enum: PENDING, SENT, FAILED, BOUNCED)`, `sent_at`, `metadata (json)`, `created_at`
Relations: may relate to WorkflowTask via metadata · may relate to OnboardingWorkflow via metadata
*Email sent via Gmail API. Status tracks delivery. Metadata stores context (workflow_id, task_id) for linking. Notification templates managed in code initially, may move to database if customization needed.*

### API contracts

| Method | Endpoint | Purpose | Auth | Confidence |
|--------|----------|---------|------|------------|
| `POST` | `/api/workflows` | Create new onboarding workflow for an employee. HR Admin initiates workflow with employee details and due date. | Google OAuth required, HR_ADMIN role | 85% |
| `GET` | `/api/workflows/:id` | Fetch workflow details including tasks, documents, and current status. Used by HR dashboard and employee portal. | Google OAuth required, role-based filtering (HR sees all, employee sees own) | 85% |
| `PATCH` | `/api/workflows/:id` | Update workflow status, due date, or other metadata. Used for manual overrides by HR Admin. | Google OAuth required, HR_ADMIN role | 80% |
| `PATCH` | `/api/workflows/:workflowId/tasks/:taskId` | Update task status (mark complete, skip, reassign). Called by task assignee or HR Admin. | Google OAuth required, task assignee or HR_ADMIN role | 85% |
| `POST` | `/api/documents/generate` | Generate document from template with provided variables. Creates Document record and triggers PDF generation. | Google OAuth required, HR_ADMIN or MANAGER role | 70% |
| `POST` | `/api/documents/:id/sign` | Initiate e-signature request for a document. Calls e-signature provider API and creates signature request. | Google OAuth required, HR_ADMIN role | 65% |
| `GET` | `/api/documents/:id` | Retrieve document content or metadata. Returns storage URL for download or inline viewing. | Google OAuth required, role-based access (workflow participant only) | 80% |
| `POST` | `/api/webhooks/esignature` | Webhook endpoint for e-signature provider callbacks. Updates document and task status when signature completes. | Webhook signature verification (provider-specific) | 60% |
| `GET` | `/api/employees` | List employees with filters (status, department, date range). Used by HR dashboard for overview. | Google OAuth required, HR_ADMIN or MANAGER role | 80% |
| `POST` | `/api/employees/:id` | Create new employee record. First step before creating workflow. | Google OAuth required, HR_ADMIN role | 85% |
| `GET` | `/api/templates` | List all active document templates. Used by HR when creating workflows or generating documents. | Google OAuth required, HR_ADMIN role | 75% |
| `POST` | `/api/templates` | Create or update document template. Admin function for template management. | Google OAuth required, HR_ADMIN role | 70% |
| `GET` | `/api/notifications` | Fetch user's notification inbox. Shows pending tasks and workflow updates. | Google OAuth required, authenticated user | 75% |

### Architecture blockers

- **E-signature Integration component API contract** — blocked by `vendor_esignature - CTO has not selected e-signature provider (confidence 0.1, blocking doubts about compliance, budget, document types)`
- **Document entity signature-related fields (signature_request_id, provider_metadata structure)** — blocked by `vendor_esignature - field structure varies by provider (DocuSign vs HelloSign vs Dropbox Sign)`
- **Webhook endpoint /api/webhooks/esignature implementation details** — blocked by `vendor_esignature - webhook signature verification and payload format are provider-specific`
- **Document Storage component vendor and configuration** — blocked by `document_storage - CTO has not decided between Vercel Blob, AWS S3, GCS (confidence 0.1)`
- **Document Service storage integration code** — blocked by `document_storage - storage SDK and API differ between Vercel Blob (fetch-based) vs S3 (AWS SDK)`
- **DocumentTemplate content_template format and variable system** — blocked by `project_scope - document complexity unknown (simple substitution vs conditional logic vs WYSIWYG), marked as blocking doubt in arch_document_generation`
- **Document generation library choice (react-pdf vs pdf-lib vs headless browser)** — blocked by `project_scope - depends on template complexity and whether HTML rendering or programmatic PDF generation is needed`
- **Audit trail entity and logging implementation** — blocked by `compliance_and_legal - audit requirements unknown (confidence 0.3), affects data model and component cross-cutting concerns`
- **Encryption at rest implementation (application-level field encryption)** — blocked by `compliance_and_legal - PII encryption requirements not specified, may need crypto library or database-level encryption`
- **Background Job Processor implementation (Vercel Cron vs dedicated worker)** — blocked by `deployment_model - CTO has not finalized hosting strategy (confidence 0.1), affects infrastructure setup`
- **Monitoring vendor selection and integration** — blocked by `vendor_monitoring - CTO has not decided on monitoring solution (confidence 0.1)`
- **Data residency configuration for PostgreSQL and document storage** — blocked by `compliance_and_legal - unknown if EU, specific region, or US acceptable for data storage`
- **Precise budget allocation for third-party services** — blocked by `budget_constraints - CEO has not provided concrete budget, CTO flagged as blocking issue`

## Risks

| Severity | Area | Description | Mitigation |
|----------|------|-------------|------------|
| BLOCKING | E-signature Provider Selection | CTO has not decided on e-signature vendor (vendor_esignature confidence 0.1 with blocking doubts). Provider choice affects API contract design, document workflow, compliance capabilities, and cost structure. DocuSign, HelloSign, and Dropbox Sign have different pricing ($1.5K-$7K annually) and feature sets. | Cannot finalize Document Service or E-signature Integration component design until vendor decided. Blockers list includes document and API contract specifics. CEO must clarify budget and compliance requirements to enable CTO decision. |
| HIGH | Document Template Complexity | Unknown whether document templates need simple text substitution, complex conditional logic, WYSIWYG editor, or government form field mapping. This affects Document Service design, development timeline (could add 2-4 weeks), and potentially requires specialized libraries or services. | CEO clarification needed on specific document types (offer letters, tax forms, I-9, etc.). For MVP, recommend starting with simple template approach and iterating. Document arch_document_generation as partial (0.6 confidence) with blocking doubt. |
| HIGH | Deployment Model Uncertainty | CTO deployment_model tension remains at 0.1 confidence. Without concrete hosting decision, cannot finalize infrastructure setup, background job strategy, or cost estimates. Vercel + Neon assumed but not confirmed. | Architect has proposed Vercel + Neon + Vercel Blob in arch_deployment_config (0.6 confidence) as reasonable default given tech stack. Needs CTO confirmation. If data residency or self-hosting required, could require architecture rework. |
| HIGH | Budget Constraints Blocking Vendor Decisions | CEO provided 'flexible but not crazy' guidance. CTO correctly challenged this (cto_challenge_budget_constraints) as insufficient for vendor selection. E-signature alone ranges $1.5K-$7K/year, hosting $600-$2.4K/year, monitoring $0-$1K/year. | Cannot select optimal vendors without budget clarity. CEO must provide concrete budget range. For now, design assumes budget-conscious choices (Neon free/hobby tier, Vercel hobby/pro, open-source monitoring) but vendors remain TBD. |
| MEDIUM | Compliance Requirements Unknown | compliance_and_legal at 0.3 confidence. Unknown if GDPR, CCPA, SOC 2, industry-specific regulations apply. Affects data model (audit trails), security implementation (encryption at rest), document storage (retention policies), and potentially vendor choices. | Assume basic security best practices (HTTPS, OAuth, encrypted storage). Design extensible audit logging hooks. If strict compliance emerges, may need architecture additions (immutable audit log, field-level encryption). Add 2-3 week buffer if compliance requirements surface. |
| MEDIUM | Background Job Execution Strategy | Vercel Cron has limitations (max 1/minute frequency, no complex scheduling, cold starts). For workflow reminders and deadline enforcement, may need dedicated worker service (adds infrastructure complexity) or accept Cron limitations. | For MVP, Vercel Cron likely sufficient (10 hires/year = low volume). Design Background Job Processor as abstraction to allow future migration to dedicated service (BullMQ, Inngest) if needed. Mark component confidence at 0.55. |
| MEDIUM | Document Storage Vendor Not Decided | CTO document_storage tension at 0.1 confidence. Vercel Blob vs AWS S3 vs GCS affects Document Service API, deployment dependencies, and cost. Data residency requirements may constrain choice. | Architect proposes Vercel Blob for simplicity given Vercel deployment assumption, with S3 as fallback. Document Service should abstract storage provider (use consistent interface) to allow swap without major refactor. Mark Document Service confidence at 0.6. |
| LOW | Mobile Experience Requirement | CTO noted uncertainty about mobile-native vs responsive web. New hires may need to sign documents on mobile devices. Responsive web is simpler but may have UX limitations for document signing and form completion. | Design mobile-responsive UI with TailwindCSS. Test document signing flow on mobile browsers early. If poor UX emerges, can add React Native wrapper in future iteration. Do not block MVP on native mobile app. |

## Discovery Questions

*Questions to ask the client before proceeding.*

1. **[CRITICAL]** What is your budget for developing this onboarding system?
   *Unblocks: budget_constraints, vendor_esignature, cto_challenge_budget_constraints, deployment_model*
2. **[HIGH]** What industry is your company in, and are there any specific compliance or legal requirements we need to consider for employee documents?
   *Unblocks: compliance_and_legal, vendor_esignature, security_model*
3. **[HIGH]** What specific documents do you need to generate and have signed during onboarding?
   *Unblocks: project_scope, arch_document_generation, compliance_and_legal*
4. **[HIGH]** Do you have any urgency or deadline for when you need this system ready?
   *Unblocks: estimated_timeline, team_needs*
5. **[MEDIUM]** Are there any existing systems or tools you want to integrate with beyond Google Workspace?
   *Unblocks: project_scope, arch_integration_strategy*
6. **[MEDIUM]** What are the key outcomes or metrics that will make this project successful for you?
   *Unblocks: success_criteria*
7. **[MEDIUM]** Do you prefer the system to be cloud-hosted or self-hosted?
   *Unblocks: deployment_model, technical_architecture*
8. **[MEDIUM]** What roles need access to the system, and what can each role do?
   *Unblocks: target_users, arch_auth_authorization*

## Inconsistencies

- **[MEDIUM]** CEO inferred a budget range based on company size, while CTO flagged this as blocking due to lack of concrete client confirmation, creating a deadlock in vendor and scope decisions.
  *Between: budget_constraints ↔ cto_challenge_budget_constraints*
- **[BLOCKING]** E-signature vendor selection is blocked by unknown budget, despite partial budget inference, leading to Architect deferring integration design.
  *Between: vendor_esignature ↔ budget_constraints*

## False Blockers

- **`compliance_and_legal`** — Doubts about GDPR or CCPA applicability are hypothetical rather than real, as the client is a US company with no evidence of international employee data handling requirements.

## Scope Reality Check

**Budget vs scope:** ALIGNED (60% confidence)

The MVP scope (workflow automation, document generation, e-signatures for 10 hires/year) aligns well with the inferred $30K-$80K budget, assuming mid-tier vendors and standard compliance.

## Epistemic Field

**28 tensions** · 5 resolved · 9 partial · Global confidence: 14%

<details><summary>Resolved tensions (5)</summary>

- **`target_users`** — who will use this system daily *(80%)*
- **`tech_stack`** — frontend, backend, and database technology selection *(80%)*
- **`vendor_email`** — email delivery for notifications *(80%)*
- **`arch_state_machine`** — workflow lifecycle and task progression logic *(80%)*
- **`qa_pipeline_coherence`** — overall coherence of the pipeline output *(80%)*

</details>

---

## Pipeline Stats

| Agent | Duration | Input tokens | Output tokens |
|-------|----------|-------------|---------------|
| CEO | 55.4s | 10 329 | 3 480 |
| CTO | 87.8s | 28 918 | 5 023 |
| Architect | 154.9s | 33 528 | 8 818 |
| QA Lead | 77.9s | 17 436 | 2 961 |

*Generated by Freed Agents · 2026-03-11*
report.client:
# HR Employee Onboarding Automation System

> A web-based application that digitizes and automates the employee onboarding process for a 50-person company. The system will replace manual paper-based workflows with automated document generation, electronic signatures, task tracking, and notifications integrated with Google Workspace. Target: reduce onboarding time from 2 weeks to 3-5 days.

## 🟡 A Few Things to Clarify

The pipeline demonstrates coherence in core scope and technical decisions, with no fundamental contradictions that invalidate the Field. However, unresolved blocking doubts on budget and compliance are causing cascading deferrals in vendor selections and architecture components, requiring client input before proceeding to presentation.

## What We Understood

**Who will use this**
- HR Manager (primary admin user)
- New Hires (form completion and document signing)
- Department Managers (approvals and notifications)

**What we're building**
- Onboarding workflow builder with customizable steps and tasks
- Automated document generation from templates (offer letters, contracts, policies)
- Electronic signature integration (DocuSign or HelloSign)
- Google Workspace integration (Gmail for notifications, Drive for document storage, Calendar for scheduling)
- Dashboard showing onboarding progress and status for all new hires
- Automated email notifications and reminders to stakeholders
- Document repository with version control and audit trail
- Task checklist assignment and tracking (IT setup, manager introductions, etc.)

**Constraints we're working within**
- Budget: estimated $30K-$80K range (needs confirmation - marked as blocking uncertainty)
- Must integrate with Google Workspace (existing infrastructure)
- Small company scale: 50 employees, ~10 new hires per year
- No dedicated IT team implied - system must be low-maintenance
- Compliance requirements unknown - industry and regulatory context not specified

## What We've Decided

- Built with Next.js 14 with React 18 and TypeScript and Node.js via Next.js API routes (unified full-stack framework), using PostgreSQL 15+ as database
- Hosted on Vercel (frontend + API routes) with Neon or Supabase PostgreSQL — Cloud SaaS, serverless functions for API, managed PostgreSQL with connection pooling, preview deployments for staging/review, environment-based secrets management
- Using Gmail API via Google Workspace for email
- Using Sentry (free tier) + Vercel Analytics for monitoring

## Questions for Our Discovery Call

1. What is your budget for developing this onboarding system?
2. What industry is your company in, and are there any specific compliance or legal requirements we need to consider for employee documents?
3. What specific documents do you need to generate and have signed during onboarding?
4. Do you have any urgency or deadline for when you need this system ready?
5. Are there any existing systems or tools you want to integrate with beyond Google Workspace?
6. What are the key outcomes or metrics that will make this project successful for you?
7. Do you prefer the system to be cloud-hosted or self-hosted?
8. What roles need access to the system, and what can each role do?

## Our Assessment

The MVP scope (workflow automation, document generation, e-signatures for 10 hires/year) aligns well with the inferred $30K-$80K budget, assuming mid-tier vendors and standard compliance.

The scope fits the estimated budget

---
*Freed Agents · 2026-03-11*
